titleStmt.title: SII 19.239: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 239. (A.R. No. 152 of 1928.) TIRUPPAḺANAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ĀPATSAHĀYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: The portion containing the king's name is damaged. This is probably an inscription of Parakēsarivarman. This is incomplete. It records an endowment of 30 kaḻañju of gold by Kiḻavaṉ Dēśappu[ga*]ḻ the queen of (the Pāṇḍya) Parāntakaṉ Mānābaraṇar, who left 22(1/2) kaḻañju of the gift with the patipādamūlattār of Āṟṟuttaḷi for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruppaḻanattu-Dēvar and 7(1/2) with ••• Nakkanaṅguḍi (?) for some purpose which is not clear.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 9Āvatu tiruppaḻaṉattu mahā
devarkku mā[�] parāntakaṉ māṉāpāṇar devi kiḻavaṉ tecappukaḻacantrāti
taval Oru noṉtāviḷakku Erippataṟku kuṭutta poṉ muppatiṉkaḻañcu Ippon
nilirupattu Iruka ḻuppaṭa Eṇṇai Aṭṭuvomānom Āṟṟut
taḷi patipātamulattom Ita kaḷ nakkaṉaṅkuṭi koṇṭa poṉ Eḻukaḻañca
rai Ippoṉṉāl nicatippaṭi laicamāṭu Eṉ paṅku muṉṟum tiruppaḻanattu
Digital edition of SII 19.239 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
